behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: classification of rename entries and aggregation into mutually exclusive categories in final report. Hypothetical elsewhere: CI shell quirks, subprocess timeouts, network, LLM quality. MOCK-MISS: this can fail at module handoff (repo-change parser -> category counter), not just inside one pure function. REAL-INFRA: behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess behavior; local repo fixture/filesystem wiring is sufficient. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: higher levels were rejected because the behavior only asks category accounting correctness, not subprocess/OS semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary git repo fixture with a true rename, run the analyzer through its normal module wiring (repo reader + classifier + report builder), and assert renamed count increments while added/deleted counts do not include that same file.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: binary-change input handling, crash resistance, and counting in report. Hypothetical elsewhere: real shell encoding edge cases on specific OSes, CI process limits, external APIs. MOCK-MISS: failure can appear when diff-reading and counting modules interact (binary marker/path representation crossing boundaries). REAL-INFRA: no explicit requirement for real OS/subprocess behavior in the behavior text; a local repo fixture with binary files and real module wiring can expose the core failure. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: System was considered, but behavior text requires binary diff robustness/counting, not OS/process behavior itself.
behaviors.test_description: Build a temp repo containing at least one binary file change, execute analyzer via its integrated code path, and assert process does not raise plus binary file appears in category/count outputs.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: no-history repository state mapping to empty report and graceful error handling. Hypothetical elsewhere: subprocess exit-code translation differences per platform, CI container oddities. MOCK-MISS: bug is commonly at boundary between repo-state probe and report generation/exception handling, so single-function unit tests are insufficient. REAL-INFRA: behavior does not explicitly require real OS/network/subprocess; local filesystem repo fixture is enough to reproduce the no-commit state. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: System rejected because expected outcome is logical handling of empty history, not explicit process-level behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a brand-new temp git repo with zero commits, run analyzer through standard internal wiring, and assert it returns an empty structured report and no thrown error/nonzero failure path.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: end-to-end correctness of counting logic against repository history across categories. Hypothetical elsewhere: OS process scheduling, remote services, model outputs. MOCK-MISS: realistic failures arise from interaction among history reader, normalization, and aggregation modules; stubbing one layer can hide mismatches. REAL-INFRA: requires real local repo history fixture, but not explicitly OS/subprocess-specific behavior per the behavior text. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: higher levels rejected because requirement is deterministic history accounting, with no explicit system or agentic dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a temp repo with a known sequence of commits producing added/deleted/renamed/modified files, run analyzer via integrated modules, and compare reported counts to independently computed expected counts from fixture setup.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: equivalence across two repository states (index vs working tree) in produced report. Hypothetical elsewhere: shell invocation differences, CI TTY behavior, network, LLM. MOCK-MISS: this is specifically a module-boundary/state-source consistency check; mocking either path can make both return the same fixture and hide divergence. REAL-INFRA: local repo/index/worktree setup is required, but behavior text does not require OS/process/network beyond local integration. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior specifies semantic equivalence of two local git states, not subprocess/OS semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Use a temp repo to create equivalent change sets once as staged and once as unstaged, run analyzer through normal module wiring for each mode, and assert the structured stdout report is byte-for-byte identical (or canonical-JSON equivalent).
